Case title: Scideator-style facet recombination for social-science prospectus ideation
Short description: Adapt facet-recombination interfaces to social-science dissertation or proposal ideation and compare human-only with human-LLM compound ideation.
Empirical unit of analysis: Prospectus ideas generated from fixed seed-paper sets
Key typology question: When does human-guided ideation still matter once novelty support is strong?
Observable artifacts / traces: Facet selections, idea sketches, novelty checks, revision notes
Key outcomes observed: Perceived novelty, feasibility, public relevance, idea diversity
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Human-LLM ideation does not outperform simple prompting or human-only ideation on meaningful criteria
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Existing Scideator evidence is not social-science-specific and may overstate transfer
Why this case has high leverage: Useful for separating novelty assistance from substantive relevance judgment.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
